title: SecID Registry Namespace
description: Validates SecID registry namespace JSON files. Each file describes one namespace (organization) within a single SecID type, including identity, resolution URLs, and pattern trees for matching subpath identifiers.

schema_version.description: JSON schema version for this file.

namespace.description: Organization identifier — domain name, optionally with /path segments (e.g., 'mitre.org', 'github.com/advisories').

type.description: SecID type this namespace belongs to.

status.description: Registry entry status reflecting documentation completeness and review state.

status_notes.description: Optional context about status (blockers, gaps, guidance for contributors).

notes.description: Free-form context for AI and human readers. Markdown allowed.

alias_of.description: If present, this is an alias stub — namespace redirects to the target value. No sources needed.

wikipedia.description: Wikipedia article URLs for direct access.

checked.description: Date someone last verified the entry is still accurate (YYYY-MM-DD).

updated.description: Date the entry last materially changed (YYYY-MM-DD).

urls.description: Top-level URLs for the organization (website, docs, API, etc.).

match_nodes.description: Pattern tree for matching SecID name/subpath components. Each node matches a portion of the SecID string and optionally has children for deeper matching.

UrlObject.description: A URL with type classification and optional metadata.

type.description: URL category: website, docs, api, bulk_data, lookup, security, security_txt, paper, github, search, secid_api, secid_mcp, etc.

note.description: Human/AI-readable context explaining what this URL is for.

format.description: Expected content format: html, json, pdf, xml, csv, zip.

checked.description: Date this URL was last verified (YYYY-MM-DD).

updated.description: Date this URL entry last changed (YYYY-MM-DD).

MatchNode.description: A pattern tree node. Matches a portion of the SecID string (name, version, subpath, or item_version) via regex patterns. Returns data when matched and optionally recurses into children for the next portion.

patterns.description: One or more regex patterns (OR alternatives). All share the same children and data. Use (?i) prefix for case-insensitive matching.

description.description: Human/AI-readable description of what this node matches.

weight.description: Match quality/preference. 0-200, higher is more preferred. Returned with results so consumers can sort.

data.description: Result data returned when this node matches. Contents vary by tree depth and SecID type — name-level nodes have source metadata, subpath-level nodes have resolution URLs. Open-ended: different types use different fields.

children.description: Child nodes for matching the next portion of the SecID string. Recursive structure.
